exampleSentences: Em không nên sờ vào đồ vật nóng vì sẽ bị bỏng.
Anh có thể sờ thử vải này xem nó mềm không không?
Trẻ nhỏ thường thích sờ mọi thứ xung quanh để khám phá.
notes:
Sờ means “to touch” with your hand, usually to feel something physically. It often implies direct contact, sometimes a bit more personal than just accidental touch.

Use it when talking about touching objects, surfaces, or people. It can feel rude or too intimate if you use it about someone’s body in the wrong context.

Close synonym: chạm (more neutral, lighter contact).
notes_md:
**Sờ** means “to touch” with your hand, usually to feel something physically. It often implies direct contact, sometimes a bit more personal than just accidental touch.

Use it when talking about touching objects, surfaces, or people. It can feel rude or too intimate if you use it about someone’s body in the wrong context.


Close synonym: **chạm** (more neutral, lighter contact).
exampleSentencesTranslated: You should not touch hot objects because you will get burned.
Can you touch this fabric to see if it is soft?
Young children often like to touch everything around to explore.
